mnemonics: B.E.D.R.O.C.K.S. (Bus, Execution, Decode, Registers, Operands, Control, Steps)
keyPoints: The instruction cycle consists of Fetch, Decode, and Execute phases
Registers are high-speed storage locations within the CPU
The control unit coordinates all activities in the CPU
The ALU performs arithmetic and logic operations
The program counter holds the address of the next instruction
commonMistakes: Confusing memory address register with memory data register
Forgetting that the program counter increments after fetch
Mixing up the roles of control unit and ALU
Assuming all instructions take the same number of clock cycles
